Take an IT assessment <%- include('partials/blogTemplate') %> Advanced Editing Techniques Color Grading: Enhancing and adjusting colors to create a specific mood or visual style. Chroma Keying (Green Screen): Removing a specific color background and replacing it with a different image or video. Motion Tracking: Tracking and attaching elements or effects to moving objects or people within a video. Transitions and Effects: Applying various transitions and visual effects to add style and continuity between video clips. Visual Effects (VFX): Incorporating computer-generated imagery (CGI) elements to enhance or create new visual elements within a video. 9 Different Types of Video Editing Linear Editing: Sequential editing on physical film or tape. Non-linear Editing: Flexible digital editing with easy rearrangement. Assembly Editing: Basic chronological arrangement for rough cut creation. Rough Cut Editing: Refining the assembly edit for coherence. Fine Cut Editing: Meticulous adjustment for polished final product. Offline Editing: Quick editing with lower-resolution or proxy footage. Online Editing: Enhancing with high-resolution footage and final touches. Montage Editing: Creating impactful sequences for music videos or promotions. Special Effects Editing: Adding visual effects and enhancements to footage. Creating quality explainer videos Step into the fascinating world of explainer videos, where creativity takes center stage. It all starts with a brilliant idea. Our creative minds craft a compelling script that weaves together storytelling and information in a seamless blend. Next, our talented artists bring the script to life. With each stroke, they illustrate captivating characters, vibrant scenes, and eye-catching visuals that will leave you in awe. Now it's time to give the video a voice. Our team infuses personality and emotion into the script, creating a lasting audio experience. Post that, our skilled animators breathe life into the illustrations. Frame by frame, they meticulously craft smooth movements, captivating transitions, and stunning visual effects. Sound is the secret ingredient that adds depth and impact. Our editors then curate a symphony of sound effects, background music, and audio enhancements that elevate the overall experience. And voilà! After careful editing and fine-tuning, the magic happens. We present to you a masterpiece, an explainer video that will captivate your audience, educate, and leave a lasting impression. So, whether you want to simplify complex concepts, introduce a new product, or showcase your brand's story, our team is here to turn your vision into an extraordinary video output. Power of VFX Advantages of using VFX: Enhances Visual Appeal Unlimited Creative Possibilities Realism and Authenticity Cost and Time Efficiency Safety and Control Brand Differentiation Power of VFX Differences Between 2D and 3D Animation Categories 2D Animation 3D Animation Dimension Flat, two-dimensional characters and environments. Three-dimensional characters and environments with depth and realism. Workflow Traditional frame-by-frame or digital vector-based techniques. Digital modeling, rigging, and animation using 3D software. Visual Style Can range from simple and minimalistic to highly detailed and stylized. Offers realistic textures, lighting, and complex visual effects. Depth and Perspective Limited depth and perspective due to the flat nature of the artwork. Allows for realistic depth, camera movements, and various perspectives. Character and Object Manipulation Characters and objects are typically manipulated in a 2D plane. Characters and objects can be manipulated in a 3D space, enabling more complex movements. Production Time and Complexity Generally quicker to produce and less complex compared to 3D animation. Requires more time and expertise due to the complexity of modeling, rigging, and rendering. <%- include('partials/popupPage') %> popup(); <%- include('partials/layoutFooter') %>

FERPA compliance is built into our data architecture from day one — not added as a layer after the fact. Student records are stored in your infrastructure, not ours. Our AI models are trained exclusively on anonymised institutional patterns and non-PII content: course catalogs, policy documents, curriculum structure, and aggregate academic patterns. Student PII never enters model training under any circumstance. We operate as a service provider under your institution's existing FERPA data governance agreements. For K-12 institutions with students under 13, we additionally build COPPA-compliant data collection and parental consent workflows into every system we deploy. What is your approach to legacy system integration? We don't require you to replace anything on a timeline that doesn't suit you. Our AI intelligence layer connects to your existing systems via API — adding intelligence without touching your operational databases. Where you want to augment your existing SIS, we build the connectors and the AI layer on top. We maintain 24/7 monitoring with US-timezone engineers — not an offshore ticket queue. What if we have limited IT staff? We designed our engagement model for institutions with lean IT teams — including single-person IT departments in smaller school districts. Phase 1 requires 2–3 hours per week from one IT Director and one data access approval. We don't require you to hire AI engineers or data scientists at any point. Justo supplies the full technical capability. Post-deployment, your ongoing commitment is one quarterly review meeting. How do you handle data migration? We run a full data audit in Phase 1, mapping every format, schema, and historical record. We build migration pipelines that run parallel to your live systems — operations continue without interruption. Every migrated record is validated against the original source before the old system is decommissioned. Student historical data going back as far as your records exist is preserved and queryable in the new system. Can you work with our existing infrastructure and cloud provider? Yes. Justo is infrastructure-agnostic. We deploy on your existing environment — your own data centre, your government cloud account, or a private cloud you control. Our open-source foundation means no proprietary dependencies that lock you to any specific infrastructure. If your institution has a pre-negotiated enterprise cloud agreement, we deploy within that agreement. What makes your AI different from what our current vendor offers? Three specific differences. First: our AI is trained on your institution's data — not generic benchmarks. We implement strict data minimisation — collecting only what is required for educational purposes. No. Areas Data Lakes Data Warehouses 1 Data Storage A data lake contains all of an organization's data in a raw, unstructured form, stored indefinitely. Structured data that has been cleaned up and processed and is ready for strategic analysis can be found in a data warehouse. 2 Users Data scientists and engineers that like to analyse data in its raw form in order to generate fresh, original business insights frequently use data from a data lake. Data visualization, BI, data analytics. 4 Schema In a data lake schema is developed after the data is collected, hastening the data capture and storage process. This method involves removing the data from its original location for storage in the data lake, and only structuring it as necessary. ETL (Extract, Transform, and Load). Data is taken from its source(s), cleaned, and then processed in this process so that it is suitable for business-end analysis. 6 Cost Storage expenses in a data lake are relatively low.
